# Project Security & Architecture Map
*(Auto-generated & continuously updated by WebSec Brief Agent)*

## 1. Identity & Stack
: Node.js runtime (versions 20-24 supported) with Express.js web framework. TypeScript/JavaScript backend with Angular frontend. Monolithic architecture with clear separation between frontend (Angular) and backend (Express/Node.js). Project structure includes standard MVC patterns with models/, routes/, lib/ utilities, config/ files, data/ for seeding, test/ for unit/integration tests, frontend/src/ for Angular code, cypress/ for E2E tests, i18n/ for internationalization, and encryptionkeys/ for cryptographic material.

## 2. Deployment & Runtime
: Startup Command: `npm start` runs `node build/app`. Entrypoint: server.ts (compiles to build/app.js). Configuration: config/default.yml for application settings, challenges, products, and features; config.schema.yml for validation. Build Steps: `npm run build:frontend` (Angular build), `npm run build:server` (TypeScript compilation), and postinstall script handles frontend dependencies. Supports Docker, Vagrant, and packaged distributions with Node.js compatibility matrix (20.x, 22.x, 24.x officially supported).

## 3. Routing
: Express.js routing paradigm with RESTful API design. Key route files located in routes/ directory (login.ts, basket.ts, order.ts, etc.). API endpoints prefixed with /api/. Frontend routes handled by Angular SPA. Static assets served from /frontend/dist (Angular build), /ftp (with directory browsing via serve-index), /encryptionkeys, and /support/logs (with directory browsing). Directory browsing intentionally enabled on /ftp and /support/logs using serve-index middleware. Custom middleware for security, logging, and challenge verification.

## 4. Security Mechanics
: JWT-based authentication using jsonwebtoken library (v0.4.0). Role-based authorization (admin, deluxe, customer) via custom middleware. Intentionally vulnerable input validation including SQL injection (routes/login.ts with raw SQL queries), XSS, insecure direct object references, security misconfiguration, and other OWASP Top Ten issues. Security headers partially implemented via helmet (v4.6.0) but intentionally misconfigured for training. Rate limiting on some endpoints. CORS enabled broadly as intentional vulnerability. security.txt and /metrics endpoints exposed. Vulnerable code snippets marked with vuln-code-snippet comments for challenge tracking. Challenge system integrated with security flaws for training purposes.

## 5. Data Layer
: SQLite database (primary) with Sequelize ORM (v6.37.3) for data access. Models defined in models/ directory (UserModel, ProductModel, BasketModel, etc.) with initialization functions. Database initialization and relations configured in models/index.ts. Supports MongoDB via MarsDB for NoSQL challenges. Raw SQL queries used in some routes for intentional vulnerability demonstration. Data caching layer in data/datacache.ts for challenges and users.

## Limitations
: Exact runtime vulnerability mapping of 100+ intentional security flaws, complete list of security challenges and dynamic flag configurations, full frontend build pipeline details, CI/CD workflow specifics, cloud deployment configurations (Heroku, AWS), complete API endpoint documentation, some route handlers and middleware logic truncated in provided source snippets, translation files (i18n/) not analyzed in detail, frontend Angular code not fully represented, encryption keys and sensitive data files not exposed in repository context.
